3. Say the word 'mop' slowly, emphasizing each sound: /m/ /o/ /p/
4. Guide child to find and place letters left to right to spell 'mop'
5. Repeat process with 'top' and 'hop', discussing the common 'op' ending
6. Have child read back each word they built by blending sounds
7. Mix up letters and let child rebuild words independently
8. Store alphabet letters in organized compartments
materials_needed: Picture cards of mop, top, hop
parent_notes: The moveable alphabet allows children to construct words before they can write fluently, supporting their natural development of spelling skills. Focus on the sounds rather than letter names, and allow the child to self-correct when possible. This concrete approach to word building prepares children for abstract reading and writing. Celebrate their efforts and don't worry about perfect letter placement initially.

slides.heading: Moveable Alphabet: Building Three-Letter CVC Words (mop, top, hop)
slides.subheading: Creating words with letters while learning the 'op' word family
slides.mascot_explanation: Hello there! I'm Betsy Bear and today we're going to be word builders! We'll use special letters to make words that all end with the same sound - 'op'!

items: Picture cards of mop, top, hop
slides.setup_instructions: Arrange the work mat in front of your child and place the needed letters (m, t, h, o, p) within easy reach.
slides.mascot_explanation: Here are all our special letter pieces that we'll use to build words! I love how each letter makes its own special sound when we put them together.

slides.text: Set out moveable alphabet letters needed: m, t, h, o, p (multiple p's) and review the sounds of each letter
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's start by getting to know our letters and the sounds they make. Remember, we say the sound the letter makes, not its name!

slides.text: Say the word 'mop' slowly, emphasizing each sound: /m/ /o/ /p/, then guide child to find and place letters left to right
slides.mascot_explanation: Listen carefully as we say 'mop' very slowly - /m/ /o/ /p/. Can you hear each sound? Now let's find those letters and put them in order!

slides.prompt: Build the words 'mop', 'top', and 'hop' using your moveable alphabet. Notice how they all end with the same sounds!
slides.mascot_explanation: Now you get to be the word builder! Take your time finding each letter and putting them together to make these special words that all sound alike at the end.

items: Can you identify the sound each letter makes?
Are you able to place letters from left to right to spell words?
Can you read back the words you built by blending the sounds?
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's see how well you can build and read your words! I'm so excited to hear you blend those sounds together to make real words.

slides.text: Read through all the words you built one more time, then organize the alphabet letters back in their proper places.
slides.mastery_check: Child can independently build CVC words and read them back using sound blending
slides.mascot_explanation: You did such a wonderful job building words today! Let's read them one more time together, and then we'll put our letters away carefully for next time.

title: Ten Board: Finding Number Combinations That Make 10
description: Children explore different ways to make the number 10 using the Ten Board and wooden tiles. This activity builds number sense and introduces early addition concepts.
instructions: 1. Present the Ten Board with numbers 1-10 printed across the top
2. Give child a container of small wooden tiles or counters
3. Show how to place tiles in the first row while counting: 1, 2, 3...up to 10
4. Remove tiles and try a different combination: 5 tiles, then 5 more tiles
5. Explore other combinations that make 10: 3+7, 4+6, 2+8, etc.
6. Let child discover and create their own combinations
7. Record favorite combinations on paper if child is interested
8. Clean up by organizing tiles and storing Ten Board properly
parent_notes: The Ten Board is a fundamental Montessori math material that helps children understand number relationships and the base-10 system. Allow your child to discover different combinations naturally through manipulation and play. This concrete experience with numbers builds the foundation for abstract addition and subtraction later. Don't rush to formal equations - let the child explore and internalize the concept first.

slides.heading: Ten Board: Finding Number Combinations That Make 10
slides.subheading: Exploring different ways to create the number 10 with tiles and counting
slides.mascot_explanation: Hi friends! I'm Betsy Bear and today we're going to discover something amazing - there are many different ways to make the number 10! Let's explore together with our special Ten Board.

slides.setup_instructions: Place the Ten Board on the work mat with the container of tiles nearby and easily accessible to your child.
slides.mascot_explanation: Here's our wonderful Ten Board and lots of little tiles to work with! The numbers at the top will help us keep track as we build different combinations.

slides.text: Present the Ten Board with numbers 1-10 printed across the top and give child a container of tiles
slides.mascot_explanation: Look at this special board with all the numbers from 1 to 10! We're going to use these tiles to fill up the spaces and make the number 10 in different ways.

slides.text: Show how to place tiles in the first row while counting: 1, 2, 3...up to 10, then explore combinations like 5+5, 3+7
slides.mascot_explanation: First, let's fill up the whole row by counting each tile as we place it. Now, let's try something fun - what happens if we put 5 tiles, then 5 more tiles?

slides.prompt: Discover as many different combinations as you can that make 10! Try 4+6, 2+8, 1+9, and see what other ways you can find.
slides.mascot_explanation: This is the exciting part where you become a number detective! See how many different ways you can make 10 using your tiles. Each new combination is like solving a puzzle!

items: Can you fill the Ten Board with exactly 10 tiles?
Can you show me different combinations that equal 10?
Can you count and identify the parts that make each combination?
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's see all the amazing combinations you discovered! I love watching you figure out these number relationships - you're becoming quite the mathematician!

slides.text: Share your favorite combination that makes 10 and explain why you liked it best. Organize tiles and store the Ten Board carefully.
slides.mastery_check: Child can independently create multiple combinations that equal 10 and recognize the relationship between the parts
slides.mascot_explanation: What wonderful discoveries you made today about the number 10! I'm so proud of how you found all those different combinations. Let's put everything away nicely for next time.

title: Montessori Bells: Matching Identical Sound Pairs with Careful Listening
description: Children develop auditory discrimination by matching pairs of Montessori bells that produce identical pitches. This activity refines the sense of hearing and introduces musical concepts.
instructions: 1. Set up 4 pairs of matching bells on separate mats (8 bells total)
2. Demonstrate how to hold and strike a bell gently with the mallet
3. Strike one bell, listen carefully, then find its matching partner
4. Show child how to test by striking both bells to confirm the match
5. Mix up the bells and invite child to find all matching pairs
6. Encourage closing eyes while listening to focus on sound alone
7. Celebrate successful matches and correct any mismatches together
8. Carefully return bells to their stand in organized fashion
parent_notes: The Montessori bells refine auditory discrimination and lay the foundation for musical appreciation. Work in a quiet environment and encourage your child to really listen rather than rushing through the activity. This sensorial experience develops concentration and attention to subtle differences in sound. If you don't have Montessori bells, you can substitute with matching sets of small glasses filled with different water levels.

slides.heading: Montessori Bells: Matching Identical Sound Pairs with Careful Listening
slides.subheading: Developing our sense of hearing by finding bells that sound exactly the same
slides.mascot_explanation: Hello there! I'm Betsy Bear and today we're going to use our ears in a very special way. We'll listen carefully to beautiful bell sounds and find the ones that match perfectly!

slides.setup_instructions: Arrange the bells randomly on the mats, ensuring there's enough space between them for your child to work comfortably.
slides.mascot_explanation: Look at these beautiful bells and the special little hammers we use to make them sing! Each bell has a partner that sounds exactly the same - can you help me find them?

slides.text: Set up 4 pairs of matching bells on separate mats and demonstrate how to hold and strike a bell gently with the mallet
slides.mascot_explanation: Let me show you how to make these bells sing! We hold the mallet gently and give the bell a soft tap - listen to that beautiful sound it makes!

slides.text: Strike one bell, listen carefully, then search for its matching partner by testing other bells until you find the same sound
slides.mascot_explanation: Now comes the fun detective work! We ring one bell and remember its sound, then we test the others until we find its perfect match. Your ears are amazing tools!

slides.prompt: Find all the matching bell pairs by listening carefully to each sound. Try closing your eyes to focus just on hearing!
slides.mascot_explanation: This is where you become a sound detective! Take your time listening to each bell and finding its twin. Closing your eyes can help you hear even better!

items: Can you strike the bells gently to make clear sounds?
Are you able to identify when two bells sound exactly the same?
Can you find all the matching pairs by listening carefully?
slides.mascot_explanation: Let's see how well your ears are working today! I'm so impressed with how carefully you're listening to find those perfect matches.

slides.text: Test all your matched pairs one more time to make sure they sound identical, then carefully place the bells back on their stand.
slides.mastery_check: Child can independently match bell pairs by sound and demonstrates careful listening skills
slides.mascot_explanation: What wonderful listening you did today! Your ears helped you find all the matching bell pairs. Let's put them away carefully so they stay beautiful for next time.

title: Read-Aloud: Chrysanthemum by Kevin Henkes - Celebrating Unique Names
description: Children listen to the story of Chrysanthemum, a little mouse who learns to love her unique name. This book explores themes of self-acceptance and celebrating differences.
instructions: 1. Show the book cover and read the title 'Chrysanthemum' by Kevin Henkes
2. Ask children to predict what the story might be about based on the cover illustration
3. Practice saying 'Chrysanthemum' together, clapping out the syllables
4. Read the story with expression, pausing to discuss Chrysanthemum's feelings
5. Stop periodically to ask how the character might be feeling and why
6. Discuss the story's message about unique names and being proud of who we are
7. Connect to children's own experiences with their names
8. End with appreciation for the beautiful illustrations and story message
parent_notes: This beautiful story addresses the important theme of self-acceptance and celebrating what makes us unique. Children may relate to Chrysanthemum's experience of feeling different or being teased about something special about themselves. Use this as an opportunity to discuss how our differences make us special and how we should treat others with kindness. The book also introduces rich vocabulary and emotional concepts that support language development.

slides.heading: Read-Aloud: Chrysanthemum by Kevin Henkes - Celebrating Unique Names
slides.subheading: Discovering how a little mouse learns to love her special, long name

slides.mascot_explanation: What do you think might happen in this story? Look at the little mouse on the cover - I wonder how she feels about her special name. Let's find out together!

slides.prompt: Listen carefully to Chrysanthemum's story and think about how she feels about her name throughout the book. Share your thoughts about the story.

slides.mascot_explanation: What a beautiful story about learning to love what makes us special! Just like Chrysanthemum, each of your names is wonderful and chosen with love by your families.
